page.content: The relationship between pain intensity and disability among individuals with lower back pain has been a focal point of recent research.

page.content: The data presented in the table offers a comprehensive analysis of various metrics, including the odds ratios and associated p-values, which provide insight into the significance of these associations. Notably, the odds ratio for the variable related to the cytoplasmic pattern of YAP1 indicates a considerable reduction in the likelihood of experiencing severe pain intensity, with an odds ratio of 0.34 and a p-value of 0.002. This finding suggests that individuals exhibiting this cytoplasmic pattern may have a statistically significant lower risk of elevated pain levels, which in turn correlates with decreased disability as measured by the Disability CPG metric. Furthermore, the EQ 5D scores included in the analysis underscore the potential impact of pain management strategies on overall quality of life for patients suffering from lower back pain. These insights are crucial for tailoring interventions aimed at improving patient outcomes in this demographic.
